title: Import Candidate Approval Persistence Schema And Adapter Implementation
objective: Implement the first repo-local Project Miner import-candidate approval persistence path using only the dedicated approval table, insert-only adapter, PM-only mutation route, stable replay identity, linked audit append, and table-backed readback classification.
active_role: Technical Repo Authority / PM Lane Coordinator / Backend Approval Persistence Executor

source_floor.meaning: Current local, origin, and Olares clean-main after PM Lane 135 current PM next actions and guardrails body controls.

allowed_scope: Add a repo migration for the dedicated seam.pm_import_candidate_approvals table.
Add a mutation-seam store adapter that inserts approval rows only into seam.pm_import_candidate_approvals.
Add a PM-only Class C POST /api/v1/mutations/project-import-approvals route.
Validate approval payloads against the existing approval contract before any insert.
Preserve idempotent replay by returning the original mutation_id and audit_event_id for identical requests.
Append exactly one linked audit event for each accepted approval insert.
Classify approval readback status from the approval table, not from audit-log-only storage.

Add focused tests for accepted insert, replay, replay mismatch, invalid payload rejection, PM/online/Class C gates, table-backed readback status, migration boundary, and existing mutation audit entity_type.
not_allowed: No Render, Vercel, or Olares deployment.
No operations-web POST wiring or approval button.
No project, workpackage, task, apparatus, issue, assignment, schedule, status, durable field record, production tracking, workbook, or import rows.
No generic mutation-pipeline registration for pm_import_candidate_approval.
No workbook macro execution or workbook writeback.
No service creation, DNS/auth/ingress/secret change, fixture replay into live data, work authorization, field release, live work order creation, or autonomous AI business-state mutation.
validation_result: PASS: py_compile passed; focused backend tests passed with 32 passed and 21 warnings; packet JSON parse returned packet-json-ok; git diff --check passed; git diff --cached --check passed after scoped staging.
sidecar_result: Read-only sidecar audit found no blocking boundary findings. It confirmed the diff stays within the dedicated approval table, PM-only route, stable replay, one-audit-append, no-import, no-frontend-POST, no-live-deploy boundary. The sidecar noted low residual risk around shared audit plumbing and insert-only enforcement; both were hardened with mutation-pipeline audit entity_type regression coverage and DB update/delete rejection triggers.
next_recommended_lane: PM Lane 137 - Approval Persistence Hosted Application Gate And Status Surfacing
